Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to synthesize the information from the five interviews to create a BPMN like textual process model Steps 1 Identify the roles 2 List the activities and their sequence including decision points and exceptions 3 Note any data objects like invoice PO email etc 4 Handle inconsistencies and note uncertainties

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Start Invoice received by email by Accounts Payable Mary Mary checks for PO number If no PO Mary sends back to supplier If PO present Mary logs the invoice into the accounting system Then Mary forwards the invoice to Purchasing Dan for confirmation of goods services received and matching with PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Dan Purchasing verifies the invoice against the PO quantities prices delivery charges If everything is correct Dan sends confirmation email back to Mary After Dan confirmation Mary matches the invoice line items against the PO in the system If the matching is okay then Mary sends the invoice to the department manager Carlos for approval If approved by Carlos then the invoice goes to Accounts Payable Mary to schedule for payment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: According to Dan if the PO number is missing I tell Mary So we have a loop here Mary sends to Dan Dan finds missing PO then Dan tells Mary to get PO then Mary gets PO and then again sends to Dan The interview does not specify so we note this as an exception that requires a loop

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We will assume that Mary logs with the PO number but Dan might not be able to find the PO in his system hence the request

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Mary says if there is a discrepancy like incorrect pricing or missing items she emails the purchasing team to clarify Sometimes if the discrepancy cannot be resolved easily she escalates it to her supervisor

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Carlos reviews the invoice with PO and confirmation If he sees something suspicious he might reject it or ask Mary to hold and investigate further If approved the invoice goes back to Accounts Payable for scheduling the payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Rita and Karen play roles in exception handling Rita steps in when Mary cannot match the invoice or Purchasing has an issue Karen is looped in by AP or Purchasing for resolving issues with the supplier

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: If the manager rejects or holds then Mary would investigate and if resolved it might come back to him Or if rejected it goes to Rita The interview does not specify but Rita role is for exceptions so we assume that if the manager rejects or holds it might become an exception handled through Rita

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After we clear up these exceptions the invoice goes back into the normal flow confirmation from Purchasing approval by the Manager then scheduling for payment So after exception resolution by Rita the process returns to the normal flow

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Given the ambiguity we will model that when an exception is escalated to Rita she handles it and then the invoice is sent back to Dan for confirmation and then to the manager

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Roles Accounts Payable Clerk Mary Purchasing Officer Dan Department Manager Carlos Accounts Payable Supervisor Rita Supplier Relationship Manager Karen Events Start Invoice received by email at Accounts Payable End Invoice scheduled for payment Data Objects Invoice Purchase Order PO Confirmation Email Approval Payment Scheduling

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process Steps 1 Start Event Invoice arrives at Accounts Payable Mary 2 Task Mary checks for PO number on the invoice Gateway PO present Yes Log invoice into accounting system and forward to Dan No Send back to supplier requesting PO and on corrected invoice restart

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3 Task Dan verifies invoice details against PO Gateway Correct Yes Dan sends confirmation email to Mary No If missing PO Dan informs Mary and Mary obtains PO then resend to Dan If discrepancy Dan may contact supplier directly or ask Mary to hold invoice until corrected version is received

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4 Task Mary matches the invoice line items against the PO in the system Gateway Match Yes proceed No Mary contacts Dan to clarify and if unresolved escalate to Rita

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 5 Task Mary sends invoice to Carlos for approval Gateway Approve Yes Carlos approves and proceed No Carlos rejects or asks Mary to hold and investigate If resolved resubmit to Carlos If not resolved escalate to Rita

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 6 Task Mary schedules the invoice for payment 7 End Event Invoice scheduled

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Exception Handling via Rita Whenever an exception is escalated to Rita she handles the exception may involve coordinating with Karen suppliers and departments After resolution Rita ensures the invoice is passed to Dan for verification then the process continues through matching manager approval and payment scheduling

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We note that there are ambiguities and uncertainties which we have tried to mark This textual model should be detailed enough for a BPMN diagram
